cards.title: Having children makes you smarter? Incredible, but true
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than their peers. Parenting demands constant mental and emotional gymnastics that may boost cognitive function.

cards.title: I was worried kids would kill my creativity
cards.body: A parent discovers that raising children sparks unexpected creative inspiration rather than stifling it. Everyday moments with kids—like a son's imaginative play—reveal a kaleidoscope of possibility.

cards.title: My holiday from hell: teenage daughter was drunk
cards.body: A family's half-term break to Cornwall turned into a 12-hour ordeal when their teenage daughter arrived at the cottage intoxicated. The chaotic journey raised serious parenting challenges around teen behavior and family dynamics.

cards.title: Sensory prom a 'perfect end' for school leavers
cards.body: Students with SEND (Special Educational Needs and Disabilities) enjoyed a specially adapted prom with quieter environments and controlled sound levels. The event provided a comfortable celebration of their school years.

cards.title: Teen who helped improve tech for disabled honoured
cards.body: Harchie Sagoo, a teen with cerebral palsy, was recognized for his work testing and improving assistive technology. His contributions helped make products more accessible for disabled users.
